step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{A}
**Clinical Reasoning:** Multiple sources indicate that effective ambulatory care coordination—including discharge planning, interdisciplinary teamwork, and chronic disease management—is closely linked to improved patient outcomes and quality of life. Specifically, models like PACE and accountable care organizations (ACOs) demonstrate lower hospitalization rates, enhanced patient functionality, and sustained quality-of-life improvements in patients with chronic illness. These findings suggest that structured, ongoing ambulatory care supports meaningful health-related quality of life outcomes.
